questions.stem: In baseball, a pitcher's earned run average is used to measure how effective the pitcher is. The earned run average, A, is calculated using the formula A = 9E/N, where E is the number of earned runs and N is the number of innings the pitcher pitched. Which equation correctly solves the formula for the variable E?

questions.image_note: Answer choices A-D are graphs. See source PDF page 3.
questions.stem: A function is shown: 7x + 4y = 14. Which graph represents the function?

questions.image_note: Answer choices A-D are graphs. See source PDF page 3.
questions.stem: An inequality is shown: 4x + 5y ≥ 10. Which graph represents the solution set of the inequality?

questions.image_note: Answer choices are number line graphs. See source PDF page 6.
questions.stem: A compound inequality is shown: 2x – 1 ≤ 3 or (1/2)x + 4 > 7. Which graph represents the solution set?
An inequality is shown: –3x + 7 ≥ –x – 5. What is the solution set for x?
What is the sum of (2x³ + 4x) and (3x³ + 5x)?
A system of equations is shown: y = 2x + 2 and 2x + 5y = 19. What is the solution to the system?
Which expression is equivalent to ∛2?
A scatter plot of the U.S. national debt from 2001 to 2016 is shown. Which statement correctly interprets the scatter plot?
An equation is shown: 3x² – 21 = 222. What is one exact solution to the equation?
Five points from a function f are plotted in the first quadrant. Which equation could represent the function?
Which expression is equivalent to (7k + 2)(5k – 9)?
A function is shown: y + 1 = 2(x + 3). Which graph represents the function?
Garrett buys a large bag of dog food. He feeds his dog 2 cups of dog food each day. Which statement describes the type of function that represents the amount of dog food in the bag after x days?
Which table could represent a quadratic function? Table A: x=0,1,2,3 f(x)=3,9,27,81. Table B: x=0,1,2,3 g(x)=–2,–4,–6,–8. Table C: x=–1,0,1,2 h(x)=6,5,6,9. Table D: x=–3,–2,–1,0 j(x)=7,7,7,7.
Which function has the greatest slope?
A system of inequalities: x < 3 and y ≥ –3. Which graph represents the solution set?

questions.topic: Domain and Range of Quadratic Functions
questions.stem: A ball dropped from 20 feet is modeled by y = –16t² + 20. What is the range of the function in terms of the situation?
Which equation models exponential growth?
Which function decays exponentially?
A line with slope –0.2 passes through (11,5). Complete point-slope form: y – [?] = [?](x – [?])
An expression is given where x ≠ 0: (4x² – 18x + 6) / (2x). Which expression is equivalent?
An equation is given: y = (x – 3)² + 1. Which type of function does the equation represent?
A function is given: h(x) = (1/5)x². How can h(15) be evaluated?

choices.A: By multiplying 1/5 by the square of 15
choices.B: By multiplying the square of 1/5 by the square of 15
choices.C: By multiplying 1/5 by the square of 15 then dividing by 15
choices.D: By multiplying the square of 1/5 by the square of 15 then dividing by 15

questions.stem: Maribel surveyed students about grade level and travel method to school. The data are shown in a segmented bar chart. Which pair of terms describes the data?
An expression is given: 42r³s² – 35r²s³. Which expression is equivalent?
The graph of a line is shown. Which equation represents the line?
Line of best fit: y = 2.33x + 92.05 where x = years since 1920. What is the projected U.S. population in millions for 2040?
Bashir has $3410 to invest in Account A (6%) and Account B (2%). He wants at least $196 interest. Which system of constraints represents the situation?
A rental company charges $2/hr for a bicycle. Which type of function models the price as a function of time?
Profit P = (200y + 23.25) – (10.53y). Which expression represents the cost of making y paintings?
Cardiac output D = V × R. Complete the equation to represent V in terms of D and R.
|x – 32| = 11. The other diver is at a depth of either ___ or ___ meters.
q(x) = 2x – 1 and r(x) table: x=0,1,2,3 r(x)=–2,4,10,16. Which statement correctly compares slopes and y-intercepts?

choices.A: r has lesser y-int and slope 2x that of q
choices.B: r has lesser y-int and slope 3x that of q
choices.C: r has greater y-int and slope 2x that of q
choices.D: r has greater y-int and slope 3x that of q

questions.stem: System: x ≥ 5 and y < –3. Which graph represents the solution set?
x² – 15x = –14. What are the two solutions?
∛18 × ∛(6^(3/5)) is equivalent to x^(3/15). What is the value of x?
Honey Mustard: Juniors=26, Seniors=22. Sweet and Sour: Juniors=11, Seniors=12. How many more students chose honey mustard than sweet and sour?
Graph of f(x) and table for g(x): x=–2,–1,0,1,2 g(x)=0.25,0.5,1,2,4. Which statement correctly compares the graphs?

choices.A: As x increases, g(x) will eventually be greater than f(x)
choices.B: As x increases, f(x) will eventually be greater than g(x)
choices.C: y-intercept of f(x) is greater than y-intercept of g(x)
choices.D: y-intercept of g(x) is greater than y-intercept of f(x)

questions.stem: 40 ≥ 6x + 2y represents hot wings (x) and soda (y) Sanjay can buy. Which graph represents all possible solutions?
Blackberries $8/lb, raspberries $9/lb, max $40. Create an inequality for pounds of blackberries b and raspberries r.
Tools worth $380 after 1 year and $361 after 2 years. Which equation represents function V?
The graph of a quadratic function is shown. Which equation represents the function?
Select all values equivalent to ∛(8/24).
Match each function type with the correct table. f(x): x=–3,–2,–1,0 f(x)=8,4,2,1. g(x): x=–1,0,1,2 g(x)=3,1,1,3. h(x): x=2,3,4,5 h(x)=1/4,1/2,3/4,1.
Business A: B(w) = 50(1.5)^w. Business B linear: w=1,$150; w=2,$200; w=3,$250; w=4,$300. Which statements correctly compare the bonus plans?

choices.A: Both offer same bonus after first week
choices.B: Business A has greater initial value
choices.C: Business A bonus is lower during first four weeks
choices.D: Both have same rate of change of 50
choices.E: Business A bonus will eventually be greater

questions.stem: The ideal gas law is pV = nRT. Which equation isolates the temperature T?
Rewrite (∛(8²³))^(3/2) · 4^(1/2) as a whole number.
Loan A simple interest increases by the same amount yearly. Loan B total grows more each year. What conclusion can Kelsey make?

choices.A: Loan A uses exponential growth because interest compounds annually
choices.B: Loan B uses exponential growth because total grows by constant percent per year
choices.C: Loan A uses exponential growth because total increases steadily
choices.D: Loan B uses linear growth because interest is added regularly

questions.stem: What is the product of 4√6 · √3?

questions.topic: Statistical Inference and Margin of Error
questions.stem: A sample of 120 students averaged 8.4 books/year ±1.2 margin of error. There are 2,000 students. Estimate the total books read.

questions.image_note: Histogram of grocery spending shown. See source PDF page 3.
questions.stem: A manager recorded spending of 23 customers in a histogram. Part A: Which pair of terms best describes this data? Part B: What is the best estimate for the mode based on the histogram?
questions.answer: Part A: C, Part B: C

questions.image_note: Distance vs time graph for Taylor's walk. See source PDF page 4.
questions.stem: The graph shows miles Taylor walks. Which statements are true?

choices.A: Taylor was at home between 11 AM and 12 PM
choices.B: Taylor was not walking between 11 AM and 12 PM
choices.C: Taylor walked at 3 miles/hr between 8–9 AM
choices.D: Taylor walked at 2 miles/hr between 3–5 PM
choices.E: Taylor's average rate between 11 AM and 3 PM was 0.75 miles/hr

questions.stem: f(x) = x² and g(x) = (x–1)² + 2. Which statement is true?

choices.A: g(x) is f(x) translated 1 right and 2 down
choices.B: g(x) is f(x) translated 1 left and 2 down
choices.C: g(x) is f(x) translated 1 right and 2 up
choices.D: g(x) is f(x) translated 1 left and 2 up

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to (x²y³z^(5/2)) / (x⁻⁴y⁵z²) for all positive x, y, z?
A manager distributes money using y = (x–200)/5, keeping $200 in a safe and giving equal amounts to 5 cashiers. What does x represent?

choices.A: Total amount at the beginning of shift
choices.B: Total amount at end of shift
choices.C: Amount each cashier has at beginning
choices.D: Amount each cashier has at end

questions.stem: Survey of 100 students: Club+Homework=28, Club+No Homework=12, No Club+Homework=30, No Club+No Homework=30. Which statement is supported?

choices.A: Club students less likely to complete homework
choices.B: No association between club and homework
choices.C: Greater proportion of club students complete homework vs non-club
choices.D: All homework completers participate in clubs

questions.stem: Which expression is equivalent to (∛27)⁴?
f(x) = 4∛x. What type of function does this represent?
Anna simple interest: yr1=$1050, yr2=$1100, yr3=$1150. Omar compound: yr1=$1050, yr2=$1102, yr3=$1158. Which statement best describes growth?

choices.A: Anna's shows linear growth because it increases by same amount each year
choices.B: Omar's shows linear growth because it grows by $50 each year
choices.C: Omar's shows exponential growth because interest added only at end of 3 years
choices.D: Anna's shows exponential growth because interest calculated on total balance each year

questions.topic: Statistical Inference and Margin of Error
questions.stem: A restaurant survey of 150 customers found average dinner spend of $28.40 ±$2.00. What does the margin of error mean?

choices.C: The true average is likely between $26.40 and $30.40
choices.D: The average includes tax and tip

questions.stem: 320 = x + 7.50(36) represents cost at a second store for 36 plants. What does x represent?

choices.A: Cost per plant at second store

questions.stem: f(x) = 3x – 1/2. If f(x) = –9 1/2, what is the value of x?

choices.B: Bike rental charges 5% of full-day rate per hour
choices.C: Ball height decreases by 70% each bounce
choices.E: Population grows by 100 people per year

questions.image_note: Box plot of jogging times. See source PDF page 8.
questions.stem: Box plot shows jogging minutes for 20 participants. Part A: Which pair of terms describes this data? Part B: Which statements are true?
questions.answer: Part A: D, Part B: A, B, D, E

questions.stem: Which student correctly solved x^(1/3) · x^(1/4)?

choices.A: Jo: x^(7/12) because exponents are added
choices.B: Kerrie: x^(2/7) because exponents are added
choices.C: Alex: x^(7/12) because exponents are multiplied
choices.D: Tracy: x^(1/12) because exponents are multiplied

questions.image_note: Answer choices A-D are graphs. See source PDF page 9.
questions.stem: f(x) = |x| – 1. If g(x) = f(x) + 2, which graph represents g(x)?
A = (1/2)s² is area of isosceles right triangle. Which equation gives the leg length s in terms of area?
Which expressions are equivalent to 2(4)^x · (4)^(2x–1)?

Survey of 9th and 10th graders (in thousands) on glasses of water per day. 9th: 0-2=10, 3-4=13, 5+=22, total=45. 10th: 0-2=19, 3-4=26, 5+=17, total=62. Part A: Complete table. Part B: Which statements are correct?
Mia: height increases 3ft/sec constantly (0,3,6,9,12,15). Jordan: height goes up then down (0,4,7,8,7,4). Which student's data is best modeled by a quadratic function?

choices.A: Mia, because height increases by constant amount
choices.B: Mia, because height increases then levels off
choices.C: Jordan, because height increases to max then decreases
choices.D: Jordan, because height increases by constant percent

questions.stem: 160 students surveyed about favorite core subject: Math=42, Science=36, English=30, History=20, Art=32. Which graphical representation is most appropriate?

questions.image_note: Graph of radioactive substance r(x) over time. See source PDF page 12.
questions.stem: Graph shows radioactive substance r(x) remaining after x hours. Part A: Average rate of change between hour 1 and hour 2? Part B: What does this represent?

part_b.B: Decreasing at avg rate of 40 mg/hr after 1 hr
part_b.C: Decreasing at avg rate of 40 mg/hr after 2 hrs
part_b.D: Decreasing at avg rate of 40 mg/hr between 1 and 2 hours

questions.stem: f(x) = 4(0.5)^x gives remaining grams of radioactive substance after x minutes. What does f(4) = 0.25 represent?

choices.D: After 4 min, 0.25 of original amount remains

questions.stem: Which graph could represent a cube function?
Tank water: t=0 → 120 gal, t=5 → 160, t=10 → 190, t=15 → 210. What is the average rate of change from 0 to 15 minutes?

choices.A: Filling more quickly every 5 min at 15 gal/min

questions.stem: f(x) = 15x – 25 is a profit function. Which statement is true of f(200)?

choices.A: Results in 15; $15 profit when 200 shirts sold
choices.B: Results in 200; $200 profit when 15 shirts sold
choices.C: Results in 2,975; $2,975 profit when 15 shirts sold
choices.D: Results in 2,975; $2,975 profit when 200 shirts sold

questions.stem: Which of the following expressions are equivalent?

T(x) = 256(1–b)^x models teams remaining after x rounds. Which statement is true?

questions.image_note: Graph of p(x) shown. See source PDF page 15.
questions.stem: Function p (graph), q: x=–2,–1,2,3,4 q(x)=–6,–4,2,4,6, and r(x) = (1.2)^x – 3. Which statements are true?

choices.B: All three increasing for all reals
choices.C: p(x) and q(x) positive when x>2
choices.D: Positive x-intercept of p(x) and x-intercept of q(x) are 1 unit apart
choices.F: As x increases, r(x) will eventually exceed p(x) and q(x)

choices.A: Circle graph — univariate categorical, shows each category vs whole

questions.stem: h(x) = 3x² + 5. How can h(4) be evaluated?

choices.D: Multiply 4 by 3, square the result, add 5

questions.stem: f(x) = 2x – 4 and g(x) = –2f(x). How is the graph of g(x) related to f(x)?

choices.A: Reflected over y-axis and compressed vertically by factor of 2
choices.B: Reflected over x-axis and compressed vertically by factor of 2
choices.C: Reflected over y-axis and stretched vertically by factor of 2
choices.D: Reflected over x-axis and stretched vertically by factor of 2

questions.stem: David earns $30/yr interest on $600. Iris earns increasing interest each year. Which best explains the difference?

choices.A: David's account grows exponentially — fixed dollar amount
choices.B: Iris's account grows linearly — increases by percent
choices.C: David's shows linear growth — constant interest amount each year
choices.D: Iris's shows linear growth — same rate of change as David

questions.stem: A = P + Prt. Which could be used to find time t?

questions.image_note: Segmented bar chart of travel method by grade. See source PDF page 18.
questions.stem: Students surveyed about travel method by grade. Part A: Which terms describe the data? Part B: Which statement is true?

part_b.C: Fewer students drive than any other method
part_b.D: Number driving is same in both grades

questions.answer: Part A: D, Part B: A

choices.B: Town population increases by 3% each year
